findings.title: Table 'credits' does not have Row Level Security enabled
findings.description: The table 'credits' in migration file 'supabase/migrations/001_create_tables.sql' does not have Row Level Security (RLS) enabled. Without RLS, any authenticated user with the anon key can read/write all rows.

findings.title: Table 'profiles' does not have Row Level Security enabled
findings.description: The table 'profiles' in migration file 'supabase/migrations/001_create_tables.sql' does not have Row Level Security (RLS) enabled. Without RLS, any authenticated user with the anon key can read/write all rows.

findings.title: Table 'settings' does not have Row Level Security enabled
findings.description: The table 'settings' in migration file 'supabase/migrations/001_create_tables.sql' does not have Row Level Security (RLS) enabled. Without RLS, any authenticated user with the anon key can read/write all rows.

findings.title: API route missing authentication check
findings.description: The API route /api/settings (PATCH) does not appear to check authentication. Any unauthenticated request can access this endpoint.

code_location.code_snippet:
1 | import { NextResponse } from "next/server"
 2 | import { getUserFromRequest } from "@/lib/auth"
 3 | import { updateRow, getById } from "@/lib/db"
 4 | 
 5 | // VULNERABILITY: Prototype pollution via JSON body
 6 | // Scanner: body_param_fuzzer (#23)
 7 | 
 8 >>> export async function PATCH(request: Request) {
 9 |   const user = getUserFromRequest(request)
10 |   if (!user) {
11 |     return NextResponse.json({ error: "Unauthorized" }, { status: 401 })
12 |   }
13 | 
14 |   const body = await request.json()
15 | 
16 |   // VULNERABILITY: Spreads entire body into preferences without sanitization

findings.title: Table 'tasks' has RLS enabled but no policies defined
findings.description: The table 'tasks' has RLS enabled but no policies are defined. This effectively blocks ALL access including the app itself. If the app works, it's likely using service_role to bypass RLS, which means RLS is not providing any protection.

findings.title: PII column without encryption: 'email' in table 'profiles'
findings.description: The column 'email' in table 'profiles' (drizzle/schema.ts) contains PII but has no corresponding encrypted column (e.g., 'email_encrypted'). Consider encrypting PII at rest to comply with GDPR/CCPA and reduce impact of a database breach.

findings.title: Dockerfile runs as root (no USER directive)
findings.description: The Dockerfile 'Dockerfile' does not include a USER directive. The container will run as root, which means a container escape vulnerability gives the attacker root access to the host. Add a non-root USER directive.

findings.title: Dockerfile exposes sensitive port 5432 (PostgreSQL)
findings.description: The Dockerfile 'Dockerfile' exposes port 5432 (PostgreSQL). Database and service ports should not be exposed from the application container. Use Docker networking instead.

findings.title: Dockerfile uses ':latest' or untagged base image
findings.description: The Dockerfile 'Dockerfile' uses '(latest tag)' without a specific version tag. This means builds are not reproducible and a compromised upstream image could be pulled. Pin to a specific version (e.g., node:20-alpine).

findings.title: Dockerfile has no HEALTHCHECK instruction
findings.description: The Dockerfile 'Dockerfile' does not include a HEALTHCHECK instruction. Without health checks, orchestrators cannot detect unhealthy containers for automatic restart.
